topic.title: DS20 Platform Identity: The IIC Completion Interrupt and the "AlphaPC 264DP" Badge

header.para: DS20 Platform Identity: The IIC Completion Interrupt and the "AlphaPC 264DP" Badge

body.para: A DS20 cold boot mis-badges itself as "AlphaPC 264DP" (SYSTYPE member 1) instead of "AlphaServer DS20" (member 6). The retire trace proves the cause is an incomplete device interface, not a cosmetic defect: EmulatR's PCF8584 IIC controller never raised the transfer-completion interrupt the interrupt-driven SRM IIC driver waits on. This topic records the proven causal chain, the interrupt routing, and the fix.
HWRPB scan reports SYSTYPE=0x22 (DEC_TSUNAMI) and SYSVAR=0x405. Member = (SYSVAR >> 10) & 0x3F = 1, which the SRM banners as "AlphaPC 264DP".
Two retire-trace captures (armed via the GuestMemory and IIC instrumentation; the per-commit trace hook requires -DEMULATR_TRACE_HOOKS=ON) establish the chain end to end:

1. get_sysvar returns member 1 with no bus traffic. In the HWRPB-build window, get_sysvar walks the open-device table and fopen("iic_ocp0") misses at the lookup stage -- zero IIC transactions -- so it never reaches a device open. build_hwrpb then OR's member 1 (1 << 10 = 0x400) into the base SYSVAR 0x5 and stores 0x405.

2. iic_init timed out registering the device. In the IIC-armed window (first node-0x40 probe), the driver issues the transfer then spins on a software completion flag in DRAM with a retry countdown while only the clock interrupt fires -- the signature of an interrupt-driven wait whose completion never arrives.

3. The firmware contract. In iic_driver.c, iic_rw_common writes IIC_START | int_flag (int_flag = IIC_ENI, 0x08), starts a 2-second timer, and does krn$_wait(&pb->misr_t.sem). The ISR iic_service (SCB vectors 0xa9/0xaa) posts that semaphore on the IIC interrupt. With no interrupt the wait hits TIMEOUT$K_SEM_VAL, sets pb->rec_count = 0, and returns 0; iic_init then skips registration (if (status != 1) continue;).

4. The model gap. EmulatR's IicPcf8584 performed the byte transfer and set the S1 PIN bit but never raised the completion interrupt: ENI was stored and ignored, and no IRQ was asserted. Hence the ISR never ran, registration was skipped, and the badge fell back to member 1.

Per the 21272 HRM device-interrupt delivery model, TIGbus interrupts set DRIR bits; DRIR & DIMn -> DIRn, and any of DIRn<55:0> asserts CPU pin b_irq<1> (device class, level-sensitive). Bits <62:58> drive b_irq<0> (errors); bit 63 is the internal Cchip error (NXM). The firmware (pc264_io.c) unmasks DIM0 bits 48, 55, and 61-63: bit 55 is the Cypress SIO (the 8259, already modeled), 61-63 are errors, and bit 48 -- marked "Unused" in the generic PCI DRIR table but deliberately unmasked -- is the DS20 Cchip IIC interrupt. The PAL maps a device bit to its SCB vector via 0x800 + vector*16.
EmulatR-only, two headers, no SSOT change:

IicPcf8584.h -- a level accessor interruptPending() = (S1 PIN == 0) && (control ENI 0x08). Derived from existing state (snapshot-clean); reads false until the guest enables interrupt mode, so the default boot is byte-identical and the polled PALcode IIC path (no ENI) is unaffected. The level-sensitive form matches HRM Table 6-9.

TsunamiChipset.h (evalDeviceIrqs) -- asserts/deasserts a Cchip DRIR bit from m_iic.interruptPending() on level change, mirroring the SIO path. The bit is selected at runtime by EMULATR_IIC_IRQ_BIT (device class 0-55), default OFF (unset) = faithful current behavior. Leading value 48; sweep 49-52 as fallback. Once confirmed it becomes a named constant.

Build RelWithDebInfo, then cold boot with EMULATR_IIC_IRQ_BIT=48 and the IIC-armed trace. Success, in increasing strength: the 0x40 wait no longer spins to timeout; GMEM-WATCH(0x2058) shows v=0x1805 (member 6) rather than 0x405; and the HWRPB scan banners member=6 "AlphaServer DS20".
See Also. Diagnostics: GuestMemory Store-Watch and Retire-Trace Arm; HWRPB Key/Value Offset Map; Virtualization Methods.
